title: ChickNGo – QR-Based Customer Loyalty & Chicken Shop Management System

requirements.text: Build a secure authentication system with a login page (login.php) and logout (logout.php). Sessions must be validated on every protected page via an auth_check include. Unauthenticated access to any protected URL must redirect to login. Display inline error messages on failed login. Default admin credentials: username 'admin', password 'admin123'.

quotes: block direct URL access without login

acceptanceCriteria: Visiting any protected page without a session redirects to login.php
Incorrect credentials show a visible error message
Correct credentials start a session and redirect to the dashboard
Logout destroys the session and redirects to login.php
Default admin (admin / admin123) can log in successfully

requirements.text: Create the admin dashboard page (pages/dashboard.php) with three summary cards: Total Customers, Today's Scans, and Total Points Issued. Data must be fetched live from the database. Layout uses a dark sidebar (#1f2933) and orange primary (#ff6b00) with white cards, soft shadows, and rounded corners to match a modern SaaS aesthetic.

quotes: Dashboard cards: Total Customers, Today Scans, Total Points Issued

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays three cards with accurate live counts
Today's Scans reflects only scans from the current date
Total Points Issued sums all points_added values in the scans table
Sidebar is visible and functional on desktop and mobile

requirements.text: Build the Add Customer module (pages/add_customer.php). The form accepts Name and Mobile. On submit: validate for duplicates (mobile must be unique), generate a unique customer_id in the format CUST + timestamp + random number, insert the customer into the DB, generate a QR code image encoding the customer_id using a PHP QR code library, save the image to /qrcodes/<customer_id>.png, and display a printable QR card preview on the same page.

quotes: Generate unique ID: CUST + timestamp + random number

acceptanceCriteria: Submitting with a duplicate mobile number shows an error and does not insert
A unique customer_id is generated on every new registration
A QR code PNG is saved to /qrcodes/<customer_id>.png
The page displays the customer's QR card preview after successful registration
All inputs are validated server-side using prepared statements

requirements.text: Build the real-time QR Scanner page (pages/scan.php) using the html5-qrcode JavaScript library. After a successful scan: extract the customer_id, fetch customer info (name, mobile, current points) via an AJAX call to a backend endpoint, and display it on-screen. Show an 'Add Points (+10)' button. When clicked, update the customer's points in the DB, insert a record into the scans table, and show a success animation/alert.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera activates on page load and scans QR codes in real time
Customer name, mobile, and points are displayed after a valid scan
Clicking Add Points increments the customer's points by 10 in the database
A scan record with points_added = 10 is inserted into the scans table
A visible success animation or alert confirms the points were added

requirements.text: Build the Customer Management page (pages/customers.php) showing a searchable, paginated table with columns: Name, Mobile, Points, and QR Code preview (thumbnail). Include a search bar that filters by mobile number in real time or on submit. Pagination should show 10 customers per page.

quotes: Table with: Name, Mobile, Points, QR Preview, Search Bar, Pagination

acceptanceCriteria: Table displays all customers with Name, Mobile, Points, and QR thumbnail
Search by mobile number filters the table results correctly
Pagination limits results to 10 per page with working next/previous controls
QR thumbnail links to or displays the generated QR image

requirements.text: Build the Offers Management page (pages/offers.php). Admin can create new offers, edit existing ones, and delete them. Each offer has: Offer Name, Required Points, and Reward Description. Use inline forms or modal dialogs for create/edit. All DB operations must use mysqli prepared statements.

quotes: Admin can: Create offer, Edit offer, Delete offer
Fields: Offer Name, Required Points, Reward Description

acceptanceCriteria: Admin can create a new offer with all three fields
Admin can edit an existing offer and save changes
Admin can delete an offer with a confirmation prompt
All offers are listed in a table on the page
All operations use prepared statements

requirements.text: Generate the complete database.sql file that creates the 'chickngo' database, all four tables (users, customers, scans, offers) with the specified schema, and inserts the default admin user (username: admin, password: admin123 stored as a hashed value).

quotes: Insert default admin: username: admin password: admin123

acceptanceCriteria: Importing database.sql in phpMyAdmin creates all four tables with correct columns and constraints
A default admin user exists and can log in with admin / admin123
customer_id in customers table has a UNIQUE constraint
All AUTO_INCREMENT and DEFAULT TIMESTAMP fields are correctly defined

requirements.text: Implement session timeout protection: if a logged-in session has been idle for more than 30 minutes, automatically invalidate the session and redirect to login.php with a timeout message.
acceptanceCriteria: Sessions idle for more than 30 minutes are destroyed on the next page load
User is redirected to login.php with a visible timeout notice

requirements.text: Apply a consistent modern SaaS dashboard visual theme across all pages: dark sidebar (#1f2933), orange primary accent (#ff6b00), white content cards with soft box shadows and rounded corners, a top navbar, and fully mobile-responsive layout. The sidebar must include navigation links to Dashboard, Add Customer, Scan QR, Customers, Offers, and Logout.

quotes: Must look like modern SaaS dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: All pages share the same sidebar and top navbar layout
Sidebar background is #1f2933 and primary accent color is #ff6b00
Cards have white backgrounds, rounded corners, and soft shadows
Layout is usable on screens as small as 375px wide
Active sidebar item is visually highlighted

constraints: Backend must use Core PHP only — no frameworks (no Laravel, Symfony, etc.)
Database access must use mysqli with prepared statements exclusively
QR code generation must use a PHP-based QR library saving PNG files to /qrcodes/
QR scanning in the browser must use the html5-qrcode JavaScript library
Project must be XAMPP-compatible and runnable by placing the folder in htdocs
Frontend must use only HTML5, CSS3, and Vanilla JavaScript — no frontend frameworks
nonGoals: Customer-facing self-service portal or mobile app
Internet Identity or any web3 authentication

required: ChickNGo app logo with a stylized chicken icon and bold text, orange and white on dark background (chickngo-logo.dim_300x80.png)
